title: RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED — Envelope consumption recorded
description:
Runtime contract for the resource event published by CAP.BSP.004.ENV whenever a budget envelope of a beneficiary records a new consumption. Every payload published on the operational RabbitMQ topic exchange owned by CAP.BSP.004.ENV with routing key EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED.RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED MUST validate against this schema (ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 Rules 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6).

Payload form: domain event DDD (ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 Rule 3) — it carries the transition data of the envelope-consumption aggregate transition: the new post-transition consumed_amount of the targeted envelope, identified atomically by (case_id, allocation_id, category) within the active period [period_start, period_end]. It is NOT a snapshot of the read model RES.BSP.004.OPEN_ENVELOPE and NOT a technical patch of fields. Consumers reconstruct the read model by aggregating the stream of CONSUMPTION_RECORDED events.

Derived consumer convenience: the available remaining balance of the envelope after this consumption is `cap_amount - consumed_amount`. This derivation is documented here for consumer convenience; it is NOT a transported field on purpose — the producer carries only the transition data, and consumers compute the remaining amount themselves to keep the wire payload minimal and avoid duplicating derivable state.

Paired business event: per ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 Rule 2, only this resource event generates an autonomous bus message. The paired business event EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED is a design-time concept (documented in EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED.schema.json) and appears on the wire ONLY as the prefix of the routing key (Rule 4) — never as a standalone message.

Identity resolution: consumers resolve case_id toward OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id via a CAP.REF.001.BEN (Beneficiary Reference) lookup. The producer (CAP.BSP.004.ENV) NEVER carries the canonical internal_id; it carries only the case_id correlation key (the participation case identifier owned by CAP.BSP.002.ENR).

allocation_id.description: Unique identifier of the targeted budget envelope (one allocation = one envelope = one (case_id, period, category) triple). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.allocation_id and RES.BSP.004.OPEN_ENVELOPE.allocation_id. Stable across the lifetime of the envelope (until period closure).

case_id.description: CORRELATION KEY. Reference to the beneficiary's participation case (Reliever case ID) owned by CAP.BSP.002.ENR. Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.case_id. Identity resolution: consumers resolve case_id toward OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id via a CAP.REF.001.BEN (Beneficiary Reference) lookup. The producer (CAP.BSP.004.ENV) NEVER carries the canonical internal_id.

category.description: Spending category covered by this envelope (e.g. ALIMENTATION, TRANSPORT, LOGEMENT, SANTE, LOISIRS). The category vocabulary is normatively held by CAP.REF.001.TIE per CPT.BCM.000.PALIER. The development stub uses an illustrative hard-coded vocabulary (see stub README); the real engine MUST source the category set from CAP.REF.001.TIE.

cap_amount.description: Maximum amount authorised on this envelope for the active period (transition reference value). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.cap_amount. Constant across the period unless a tier upgrade triggers a recalibration (which produces a new RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_INITIALIZED event — outside the scope of this schema).

consumed_amount.description: Total consumed amount of this envelope AFTER applying the recorded consumption (transition target value). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.consumed_amount. Domain-event-DDD field — carries the post-transition state of the consumption counter, not the delta. Always satisfies 0 <= consumed_amount; reaching cap_amount triggers a paired RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CAP_REACHED event in the same transaction (out of scope here, see the process model invariant INV.ENV.004). Available remaining balance is computable by consumers as `cap_amount - consumed_amount` — NOT transported on purpose.

period_start.description: Inclusive start date of the envelope's active allocation period (ISO-8601 calendar date, YYYY-MM-DD). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.period_start. Stable across the period. Carried so consumers can scope their projections to the active period without a separate lookup.

period_end.description: Inclusive end date of the envelope's active allocation period (ISO-8601 calendar date, YYYY-MM-DD). Mirrors OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.period_end. Stable across the period. Marks the boundary after which the envelope transitions to closure (RVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_PERIOD_WITHOUT_CONSUMPTION if not consumed) — outside the scope of this schema.
